engineGuide.h2: Jaguar XK Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jaguar XK across all generations (X100 1996-2005 and X150 2006-2014), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: The original Jaguar V8 introduced for the 1997 XK8. Early Nikasil cylinder bore lining proved vulnerable to high-sulphur UK fuel.
commonFailures: Nikasil cylinder bore scoring (high-sulphur fuel damage, typically 50k-80k miles)
Timing chain tensioner failure (plastic components)
Valve stem seal degradation causing blue smoke on startup
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ26 Jaguar XK engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Revised version of the AJ-V8 introduced for the 1998 model year. Steel cylinder liners replaced Nikasil, solving the bore scoring crisis.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (plastic shoes crack, typically 60k-90k miles)
Water pump bearing failure
Throttle body motor failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ27 Jaguar XK engine replacement

entries.title: 4.2L Petrol
entries.history: The Gen III AJ-V8 introduced at the 2009 facelift. Direct injection and 11.5:1 compression delivered 385bhp with improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: XK 4.2 Coupe, XK 4.2 Convertible, XK Portfolio 4.2
XK 4.2 Coupe, XK 4.2 Convertible, XK Portfolio 4.2
XK 5.0 Coupe, XK 5.0 Convertible, XK Portfolio 5.0, XK Signature 5.0, XK Dynamic R 5.0, XK Ultimate 5.0
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (primary failure driver, typically 50k-80k miles)
VVT solenoid failure causing rough idle
Cooling system leaks (water pump, thermostat housing)
Timing chain tensioner failure
Secondary timing chain stretch
Camshaft actuator wear
Oil pump drive failure (Jaguar Recall JAG-19V-005, typically 40k-70k miles)
Cylinder bore scoring causing oil consumption (1L/1000 miles)
Water pump failure (revised 2012 design still vulnerable)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 508PN Jaguar XK engine replacement

entries.title: 4.2L Supercharged Petrol
entries.history: The first supercharged XK engine, fitted to the XKR from 2006. An Eaton M112 supercharger lifted output to 420bhp with instant torque.
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler wear (rattling noise, progressive boost loss, 40k-70k miles)
Timing chain tensioner failure (identical to naturally aspirated AJ34)
Intercooler pump failure causing heat soak
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ34S Jaguar XK engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Supercharged Petrol
entries.history: The Gen III supercharged V8 introduced with the 2010 XKR. An Eaton Twin Vortices Series supercharger delivered 510bhp and 461lb-ft torque.
compatibleVariants: XKR 5.0 Coupe, XKR 5.0 Convertible, XKR 75 5.0, XKR-S 5.0 Coupe, XKR-S 5.0 Convertible, XKR-S GT 5.0
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler wear (front bearing rattle, boost loss, 40k-60k miles)
Oil pump drive failure (Recall JAG-19V-005 affects all Gen III)
Injector seal failure (direct injection carbon buildup)
Timing chain tensioner wear (revised components in later units)

entries.title: 5.0L Supercharged Petrol (High Output)
entries.history: The high-output variant of the 5.0 supercharged V8. Distinguished by revised ECU mapping delivering 550bhp in XKR-S and XKR-S GT applications.
compatibleVariants: XKR-S 5.0 Coupe, XKR-S 5.0 Convertible, XKR-S GT 5.0
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler wear (same design as AJ133)
Oil pump drive failure (Recall JAG-19V-005)
High-pressure fuel pump failure

entries.title: 4.0L Straight-Six Petrol
entries.history: The final evolution of Jaguar's legendary XK straight-six. Fitted to the 1996-1997 XK8 before the AJ-V8 arrived. A robust, non-interference design.
commonFailures: Cylinder head gasket failure
Cam position sensor failure
Distributor cap and rotor corrosion

commonProblems.h2: Jaguar XK Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Jaguar XK Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar XK Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why AJ-V8 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioner shoes become brittle with heat cycles and oil contamination, then crack or shatter. Chain slack follows, skipping teeth on cam sprockets, leading to valve-to-piston contact. The 4.2L engines use a single primary chain with two secondary chains; 5.0L Gen III engines use a revised layout with similar tensioner vulnerability.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Timing Chain Replacement - Replace tensioners & chains before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain set replacement with upgraded steel-caged tensioners, new guides, cam sprockets, and crankshaft bolt
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles. Only viable if engine has NO rattling or fault codes. Prevents catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.tier: Post-Failure Repair - After chain slip but before piston contact
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing components + cylinder head removal, valve inspection, camshaft replacement if damaged
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - hidden bottom end damage may surface later. Often a gamble vs full replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, new gaskets, oil pump, bench tested before dispatch
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles expected. Includes 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. All known weak points addressed.

problems.recommendation: If you catch the rattle early and have no fault codes, preventative replacement at a specialist (£1,800-£2,800) is excellent value. Once the chain has slipped and you're looking at post-failure repair (£3,000-£4,500), we recommend going straight to a rebuilt engine. The labour overlap is significant, and the repaired engine still carries risk of hidden bottom end damage. On an XK worth £8,000-£15,000, a specialist rebuilt engine with warranty is the financially sound decision.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ-V8 engine prices for your Jaguar XK

problems.h4: Jaguar XKR Supercharger Coupler Failure - Why Supercharged V8s Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: The Eaton supercharger uses a nylon/plastic coupler between the snout shaft and rotor pack. Heat cycling and torsional stress cause the coupler to wear down, creating a rattle at idle and progressive boost loss. The 4.2L XKR uses an M112 unit; 5.0L XKR uses a Twin Vortices Series (TVS) supercharger - both share coupler vulnerability.

repairOptions.tier: Supercharger Coupler Replacement - New coupler + snout service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supercharger removal, snout disassembly, new coupler, fresh oil, needle bearing inspection
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles. Only viable if supercharger bearings are intact. Rattling noise only - no boost loss yet.

repairOptions.tier: Full Supercharger Rebuild - Coupler + bearings + seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supercharger strip-down, new coupler, front and rear bearings, seals, rotor timing verification
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles. Required if rattling has progressed to bearing wear or boost loss.

repairOptions.tier: Full Engine Replacement - Rebuilt unit with upgraded coupler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine including fully serviced supercharger with upgraded coupler, new timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles. The only option if coupler failure caused debris ingestion or engine damage.

problems.recommendation: Replace the coupler the moment you hear rattling from the front of the supercharger. At specialist prices (£700-£1,200), it's preventative maintenance that protects a £2,000-£3,000 supercharger. If you've already lost boost or hear grinding, opt for the full supercharger rebuild (£1,400-£2,200). XKR owners who ignore the rattle often face engine replacement when coupler debris circulates or bearings fail catastrophically.
problems.cta: Get quotes for XKR supercharged engine replacement with upgraded couplers

problems.group: Jaguar XK Oil Pump Drive Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar XK Oil Pump Drive Failure - Recall JAG-19V-005 Explained
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XK 5.0 (2010-2014, 508PN), XKR 5.0 (2010-2014, AJ133), XKR-S 5.0 (2011-2014) - all Gen III AJ-V8 engines
problems.rootCause: The oil pump drive sleeve (connecting the crankshaft to the pump rotor) fractures due to inadequate material strength and harmonics. Oil pressure drops to zero without warning. The engine seizes within minutes. Jaguar issued Recall JAG-19V-005, but many cars never received the updated pump.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Oil Pump Replacement - Before failure occurs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of oil pump assembly with revised JAG-19V-005 spec unit, new drive sleeve, sump gasket, oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with revised component. Only viable if engine has NO oil pressure warning and NO knocking.

repairOptions.tier: Post-Failure Engine Repair - After seizure but before catastrophic damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Crank regrind or replacement, new bearings, oil pump, full strip and rebuild
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - crank damage is often worse than initial inspection reveals. High risk of repeat failure.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with revised oil pump drive, new timing components, fully tested
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles. The only reliable option if the engine has seized or thrown a rod. 12-month warranty included.

problems.recommendation: If your 5.0L XK has no record of Recall JAG-19V-005 being performed, spend the £900-£1,400 on preventative oil pump replacement at a specialist. It's the cheapest insurance you can buy for a Gen III AJ-V8. Once the pump fails and the engine seizes, replacement is your only reliable path. The post-failure repair cost approaches replacement cost with inferior outcome.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 5.0L XK engine prices with upgraded oil pump drive

problems.h4: Jaguar XK Cylinder Bore Scoring - Why 5.0L Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XK 5.0 (2010-2014, 508PN), XKR 5.0 (2010-2014, AJ133) - predominantly 2010-2012 production
problems.rootCause: Direct injection on the Gen III 5.0L AJ-V8 leaves intake valves without fuel washing, allowing carbon buildup. However, bore scoring is the more serious failure - cylinder walls develop vertical scoring marks, usually on cylinders 4, 5, and 8. Oil consumption climbs to 1L per 1,000 miles or worse. Piston ring collapse and poor cylinder liner material are suspected contributors.

repairOptions.tier: Carbon Cleaning & Oil Consumption Test - Diagnostic only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting of intake valves, compression test, oil consumption measurement over 1,000 miles
repairOptions.longevity: Confirms diagnosis only. Does not fix bore scoring. Use before committing to replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Engine Rebuild - Strip, rebore, new pistons
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine strip, cylinder rebore or resleeve, oversize pistons and rings, new bearings, gaskets, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles if properly machined. Only viable if crank and heads are undamaged.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt unit with upgraded liners
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with cylinder liners verified or replaced, new piston rings, upgraded oil pump, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles. Includes 12-month warranty. More reliable than a rebuild of your scored block.

problems.recommendation: Bore scoring is progressive - it won't improve. If oil consumption is under 1L per 1,000 miles, monitor it. Once it exceeds that, replacement is usually better value than rebuilding your existing engine. A specialist rebuilt replacement engine (from £6,000 supply & fit) comes with warranty and fully inspected cylinder bores. On a 5.0L XK worth £12,000-£18,000, don't cut corners - replace with a warranted unit.
problems.cta: Compare 5.0L Jaguar XK rebuilt engine prices with bore scoring warranty coverage

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jaguar XK
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain failure, supercharger coupler wear, oil pump collapse, and bore scoring are the four biggest threats to your XK's AJ-V8. But none of them need to end your ownership. EngineMarket connects you with UK Jaguar specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components - addressing every factory weakness at source. All units carry a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery is available, and supply & fit options remove the hassle. Compare prices today and get your XK back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jaguar XK Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jaguar XK and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jaguar XK Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jaguar XK across both X100 and X150 generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: Jaguar XK 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L AJ-V8 launched the XK8 generation (X100, 1996-2005) and remained the sole engine option until the 4.2L arrived in 2003. Early Nikasil-lined AJ26 units suffered bore scoring issues on high-sulphur UK fuel, while later AJ27 engines switched to steel liners. These engines defined Jaguar's return to V8 grand touring.
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XK 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar XK 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2L AJ-V8 arrived with the X150 generation in 2006, offering 300bhp in naturally aspirated form. This displacement powered the standard XK Coupe and Convertible, as well as the Portfolio trim. Known for robust torque delivery, the 4.2L remains the most popular naturally aspirated XK engine on the used replacement market.
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XK 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar XK 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Gen III 5.0L AJ-V8 arrived with the 2009 facelift, bringing direct injection and 385bhp naturally aspirated. Supercharged XKR and XKR-S variants produced 510-550bhp. This displacement dominated X150 production from 2010-2014 and remains the highest-volume replacement engine for the XK range, though oil pump drive failures and bore scoring are well-documented risks. Not sure which engine size your Jaguar XK needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Jaguar XK specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Jaguar XK Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Jaguar XK engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the early XK8 4.0L V8 to the final XKR-S 5.0L supercharged grand tourer.

items.title: Jaguar XK Petrol Engines
items.description: The Jaguar XK was powered exclusively by petrol V8 and straight-six engines across both X100 (1996-2005) and X150 (2006-2014) generations, with naturally aspirated and supercharged options dominating replacement demand.
items.descriptor: The Jaguar XK was powered exclusively by petrol V8 and straight-six engines across both X100 (1996-2005) and X150 (2006-2014) generations, with naturally aspirated and supercharged options dominating replacement demand.
families: AJ26 / AJ27 - 4.0L V8 (X100 XK8, 1996-2005), Nikasil bore scoring on early units
AJ16 - 4.0L straight-six (early X100 XK8, 1996-1997), robust non-interference design
AJ34 / 5G - 4.2L V8 (X150 XK, 2006-2009), timing chain tensioner failure primary driver
AJ34S - 4.2L supercharged V8 (X150 XKR, 2006-2009), coupler wear plus chain issues
508PN - 5.0L V8 (X150 XK, 2010-2014), oil pump drive recall JAG-19V-005
AJ133 / AJ133S - 5.0L supercharged V8 (X150 XKR/XKR-S, 2010-2014), coupler and pump failures
foundIn: X100 XK8 (1996-2005) - 4.0L naturally aspirated V8 (AJ26/AJ27) + 4.0L straight-six (AJ16)
X150 XK (2006-2014) - 4.2L and 5.0L naturally aspirated V8 (AJ34/5G, 508PN)
X150 XKR (2006-2014) - 4.2L and 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ34S, AJ133)
X150 XKR-S (2008-2014) - 4.2L and 5.0L high-output supercharged V8 (AJ34S, AJ133S)
knownFor: Glorious V8 soundtrack and effortless grand touring torque delivery
Timing chain tensioner failure across all AJ-V8 generations (50k-85k miles)
Supercharger coupler rattling on XKR models (40k-70k miles)
Oil pump drive failure on 5.0L Gen III engines (Recall JAG-19V-005)

engineYears.h2: Jaguar XK Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: The Jaguar XK spanned two generations (X100 1996-2005 and X150 2006-2014) with three distinct V8 displacements - below is a complete year-by-year technical timeline to help owners identify their exact engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: X100 Early Production: AJ16 Straight-Six & AJ26 V8 Launch
years.description: The XK8 launched with two dramatically different engines - the final evolution of Jaguar's legendary straight-six and the all-new AJ-V8 that would define the brand for two decades.
keyChanges: Launch of X100 XK8 Coupe and Convertible
AJ26 4.0L V8 (290bhp) with Nikasil bore lining
First Jaguar V8 in a production car
knownFor: AJ16 is bulletproof but rare - only fitted for 18 months
AJ26 Nikasil liners fail on high-sulphur UK fuel (50k-80k miles)
Early XK8s are appreciating classics with specialist engine support
Straight-six cars are the rarest XK variant ever sold in the UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-1997 Jaguar XK8 replacement engines

years.preview: X100 Steel Liners: AJ27 V8 Arrives
years.description: Jaguar switched to steel cylinder liners on the AJ27, solving the Nikasil bore scoring crisis that plagued early XK8s.
keyChanges: AJ27 4.0L V8 replaces AJ26 with steel cylinder liners
XK8 remains sole variant (XKR launched 1999 in next block)
knownFor: Steel liners permanently fix bore scoring concerns
Timing chain tensioner remains plastic - still fails 60k-90k miles
More reliable than early cars but still needs preventative chain work
Strong classic following with good parts availability
years.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ27 engine prices for your XK8
years.ticker: Jaguar XK8 engine replacement • AJ27 engine rebuild cost • XK8 timing chain failure

years.preview: X100 XKR Arrives: Supercharged V8 Debuts
years.description: The XKR arrived with an Eaton supercharger on the 4.0L AJ27S, launching Jaguar's performance grand tourer lineage.
keyChanges: Eaton M112 supercharger added to AJ27 (AJ27S designation)
knownFor: First supercharged XK - instant torque and distinctive whine
Supercharger coupler wear less common than later X150 generations
Timing chain tensioner still vulnerable across all AJ27 variants
XKR commands significant premium over XK8 on used market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2002 XKR supercharged engine replacement

years.description: The 4.2L AJ-V8 replaced the 4.0L across the X100 range, delivering 294bhp in XK8 and 400bhp in XKR with improved torque.
keyChanges: Final years of X100 generation before X150 launch
knownFor: Last of the X100 generation - most refined of the early cars
4.2L engines share same timing chain weakness as 4.0L
XKR 400bhp still feels genuinely quick today
Lower production numbers than X150 - parts availability moderate
years.cta: Compare 4.2L Jaguar XK engine prices for 2003-2005 models

years.description: The all-new X150 generation launched with bonded aluminium construction and the familiar 4.2L AJ-V8 in both naturally aspirated and supercharged forms.
keyChanges: Completely new X150 platform - aluminium monocoque
knownFor: Timing chain tensioner failure very common (50k-80k miles)
XKR supercharger coupler rattle appears from 40k miles
Most affordable XK generation to buy - high replacement engine demand
Pre-facelift cars need upgraded tensioners as preventative work
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2008 Jaguar XK 4.2 replacement engines
years.ticker: Jaguar XK 4.2 timing chain failure • XKR supercharger coupler replacement • XK 4.2 engine rebuild cost

years.description: The XKR-S arrived as a limited-run 4.2L supercharged variant with 420bhp, revised suspension and unique calibration.
keyChanges: XKR-S introduced (2008, only 200 coupes for UK)
Same AJ34S engine as standard XKR
ECU remap not required - identical output
knownFor: Rare - only 200 UK cars produced
No additional engine power over standard XKR
Collectors' item with specialist engine support
Same timing chain and coupler vulnerabilities as standard XKR
years.cta: Enquire about XKR-S 4.2 engine availability (limited production)

years.description: The critical changeover year - early 2009 cars have the 4.2L, late 2009 cars receive the new 5.0L Gen III engine. Check your VIN before ordering.
keyChanges: Late 2009: Facelift arrives with 5.0L Gen III AJ-V8
knownFor: Do not assume engine size by registration year alone
5.0L brings direct injection and new failure modes
Check engine code stamp before ordering any replacement
Critical year for compatibility research
years.cta: Verify your 2009 XK engine code and compare replacement prices
years.ticker: Jaguar XK 2009 engine compatibility • 4.2L vs 5.0L XK engine • which engine does my 2009 XK have

years.preview: X150 5.0L Gen III Era: Direct Injection Arrives
years.description: The 5.0L Gen III AJ-V8 becomes standard across the XK range, with direct injection delivering 385bhp naturally aspirated and 510bhp supercharged.
keyChanges: All XK models now 5.0L Gen III V8 only
Direct injection on naturally aspirated units
Revised front-end styling and interior updates
knownFor: Oil pump drive failure (Recall JAG-19V-005) - widespread issue
Supercharger coupler wear continues on XKR models
Cylinder bore scoring potential on high-mileage units
Most common XK generation on UK roads - highest replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Jaguar XK 5.0 replacement engines
years.ticker: Jaguar XK 5.0 oil pump failure • XKR 5.0 engine replacement cost • 5.0L Gen III timing chain issues

years.description: The ultimate XK arrived with 550bhp from a high-output version of the 5.0L supercharged AJ133, badged AJ133S for the XKR-S.
keyChanges: Revised ECU mapping and exhaust system
knownFor: Most powerful Jaguar XK ever produced
Same oil pump vulnerability as standard AJ133
Supercharger coupler wear still present
Limited production - specialist sourcing required for replacement
years.cta: Enquire about XKR-S 5.0 replacement engine availability

years.preview: X150 Final Production Years: Run-Out Models
years.description: The final years of XK production saw special editions including the XKR-S GT, XK Signature, and XK Dynamic R before the model was discontinued.
keyChanges: XKR-S GT launched (2013-2014, only 25 UK cars)
XK Signature and XK Dynamic R trims added
No mechanical engine changes from 2010-2012 units
mainEngines: 508PN - 5.0L petrol V8 (XK, XK Signature, XK Dynamic R)
AJ133S - 5.0L supercharged petrol V8 (XKR-S, XKR-S GT)
knownFor: Latest XK engines - but 5.0L failures still present
Some late cars received revised water pump design
Last chance to buy a new Jaguar grand tourer V8
Well-maintained cars hold value better than earlier years
years.cta: Compare replacement engine prices for 2013-2014 Jaguar XK models
years.ticker: Jaguar XK 2013 engine replacement • XKR-S GT engine availability • 2014 XK final edition engine

faq.h2: Jaguar XK Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Jaguar XK 4.2 engine reliable, or should I get the 5.0?
items.answer: The 4.2L AJ-V8 is very reliable when its timing chain tensioners are upgraded. The 5.0L Gen III offers more power but adds oil pump failure risks. Both are good choices with different trade-offs.
keyPoints: 4.2L's main weakness is plastic timing chain tensioners - upgrade to steel-caged units
5.0L delivers 385bhp (or 510bhp supercharged) but has oil pump drive failures
5.0L engines also suffer cylinder bore scoring at higher mileages
4.2L generally cheaper to rebuild - from £3,895 vs 5.0L from £4,500
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 4.2L and 5.0L Jaguar XK engine prices side by side

items.question: How much does a Jaguar XK engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt 4.2L XK engine costs £3,895-£5,500 supply only. XKR 5.0 supercharged units run £5,900-£7,800. Add £1,200-£2,200 for professional supply and fit through a specialist.
keyPoints: 4.2L naturally aspirated: £3,895-£5,500 (AJ34, 5G codes)
4.2L supercharged (XKR): £5,500-£7,500 (AJ34S)
5.0L naturally aspirated: £4,500-£6,200 (508PN)
5.0L supercharged (XKR/XKR-S): £5,900-£8,500 (AJ133/AJ133S)
Supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,200 depending on XK variant
items.cta: Get an instant Jaguar XK engine replacement quote from UK specialists

items.question: What are the common signs my Jaguar XK engine is failing?
items.answer: Rattle on startup points to timing chain tensioner failure. Rattling from the front of a supercharged XKR suggests coupler wear. Blue smoke indicates valve stem seals. Knocking means bottom end bearing failure - stop driving immediately.
keyPoints: Rattle on cold start that lasts 1-2 seconds = timing chain tensioners failing
Rattling noise from engine front that changes with revs = XKR supercharger coupler wear
Blue smoke on startup = valve stem seals (progressive, not catastrophic)
Persistent knocking = rod or main bearing failure - do not drive
Check engine light with VVT codes = solenoid failure, often repairable without full replacement
items.cta: Book a diagnostic or compare replacement engine prices for your XK

items.question: Can I fit an XKR supercharged engine into my standard XK?
items.answer: Not a direct swap. You would need the XKR ECU, complete engine harness, cooling system upgrades, transmission modifications, and a different differential. It is prohibitively expensive.
keyPoints: Different ECU mapping - XKR uses supercharger-specific calibration
Engine harness is not compatible between naturally aspirated and supercharged
Cooling system requires upgrades for supercharger heat rejection
Transmission and differential ratios differ between XK and XKR
Most specialists recommend selling your XK and buying an XKR instead
items.cta: Compare prices on XKR complete engines if you are set on the swap

items.question: Does the Jaguar XK timing chain need regular replacement?
items.answer: Jaguar does not specify a timing chain replacement interval. However, the plastic tensioners typically fail between 50,000-85,000 miles. Preventative replacement is strongly recommended on all AJ-V8 engines.
keyPoints: Tensioner failure symptoms: rattle on startup, metallic noises, check engine light
Failed tensioners allow chain to skip teeth - causes valve and piston contact
Preventative replacement costs £1,800-£2,800 at a specialist
Post-failure repair often costs more than a rebuilt engine
Upgraded steel-caged tensioners should be fitted during any replacement
items.cta: Compare rebuilt XK engines with upgraded timing components included

items.question: Which Jaguar XK engine should I avoid when buying used?
items.answer: The 5.0L Gen III (2010-2012) has the worst reputation due to oil pump drive failures (Recall JAG-19V-005) and bore scoring. The 4.2L is more robust but still needs timing chain tensioner upgrades.
keyPoints: 2010-2012 5.0L cars most affected by oil pump failure - check recall was performed
4.2L timing chain tensioners fail - budget for preventative replacement on any used XK
XKR supercharged models have additional coupler wear costs (£700-£1,200)
Late 4.2L (2008-2009) and post-recall 5.0L (2013-2014) are safest bets
Full service history is essential - missed oil changes accelerate all failure points
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt engine if you already own an affected XK

items.question: How long does a Jaguar XK engine last before needing replacement?
items.answer: With proper maintenance and preventative timing chain work, an AJ-V8 can reach 150,000-200,000 miles. However, most original engines fail between 60,000-100,000 miles due to tensioner or oil pump failure.
keyPoints: Well-maintained 4.2L engines regularly exceed 150,000 miles after tensioner upgrade
5.0L Gen III with recall performed can also reach high mileages
Oil changes every 5,000-7,000 miles (not the 15,000-mile Jaguar schedule) extend life
Most XK engines on replacement market have failed between 60k-100k miles
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components often outlast original factory units
items.cta: Compare prices on long-block rebuilt XK engines with upgraded internals

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding my Jaguar XK engine or should I replace it?
items.answer: Replace if timing chain failure caused valve/piston damage, cylinder bore scoring is present, or crankshaft has failed. Rebuild only for minor issues like valve stem seals or head gasket failure without other damage.
keyPoints: Catastrophic failure (snapped chain, seized oil pump) = cheaper to replace than rebuild
Bore scoring on 5.0L = replacement with warranted unit is safer than reboring your block
Minor oil leaks or valve seals = rebuild is viable and more cost-effective
Rebuilt replacement engines come with 12-month warranty - rebuilds of your engine often do not
Most specialists recommend replacement over rebuild for XK V8s
items.cta: Compare rebuilt XK engine prices against rebuild quotes for your engine

items.question: Does the Jaguar XK have a diesel engine option?
items.answer: No. Every Jaguar XK sold in the UK (both X100 XK8 and X150 XK/XKR) uses a petrol V8 or, on the earliest XK8 models, a 4.0L straight-six. There is no diesel XK.
keyPoints: X100 XK8: 4.0L straight-six (AJ16) or 4.0L/4.2L V8 (AJ26/AJ27/AJ34)
X150 XK: 4.2L or 5.0L naturally aspirated V8 (AJ34/5G, 508PN)
X150 XKR: 4.2L or 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ34S, AJ133/AJ133S)
All Jaguar XK engines require 5W-20 or 5W-30 fully synthetic oil
No diesel variant was ever produced for any global market
items.cta: Compare petrol replacement engines for your Jaguar XK

items.question: What oil does a Jaguar XK V8 need, and how often should I change it?
items.answer: Jaguar specifies 5W-20 fully synthetic oil for the AJ-V8 family. Change every 5,000-7,000 miles for maximum protection - ignore the 15,000-mile service schedule if you want the engine to last.
keyPoints: 5W-20 fully synthetic is the manufacturer specification for all AJ-V8 engines
Oil capacity is approximately 7.3 litres (7.7 quarts) for the V8
5W-30 is also acceptable for higher-mileage engines or warmer climates
Extended oil change intervals contribute to timing chain tensioner failure
Use genuine Jaguar filters or high-quality OEM equivalents
items.cta: Compare prices on rebuilt XK engines - proper oil history affects long-term reliability
